headline_metrics.revenue: $7.3 billion total net revenue, up 4.7% year over year
headline_metrics.roe_rotce: Return on tangible common equity of 17%; return on average assets of 1.15%

nim_nii.summary: NIM was flat at 2.77% as core loan growth and stable deposit pricing offset elevated mortgage prepayments and tighter spreads; management expects continued margin progression and reiterated a path to 3%, with full-year NII growth guided to 4%-6%.
nim_nii.quote: Net interest margin was flat linked quarter at 2.77%, as core loan growth and stable deposit pricing were offset by elevated mortgage prepayments and somewhat tighter credit spreads.
nim_nii.speaker: John Stern, CFO

credit_normalization.summary: Credit quality remained stable with nonperforming assets improving to 0.38%; the net charge-off ratio ticked up 2 bps to 0.56% driven by normal credit card seasonality, with management characterizing client resilience as strong.
credit_normalization.quote: The first quarter net charge-off ratio was 0.56%, increasing 2 basis points sequentially, driven by the seasonal nature of credit cards, while our allowance for credit losses of nearly $8 billion represented 2% of period-end loans.
credit_normalization.speaker: John Stern, CFO

cre_reserves.summary: Management did not specifically discuss CRE loan-loss reserve builds or releases or office CRE; CRE was only cited as a contributor to broad-based loan growth, and the overall allowance was held at nearly $8 billion (2% of loans).

deposit_costs.summary: Deposit pricing was stable with the deposit mix improving as record consumer deposits offset wholesale seasonality; management is reducing CDs and higher-cost institutional deposits and expects to hold the line on costs.
deposit_costs.quote: On deposits, yes — we have seen stabilization in deposit mix. We are ultra-focused on growing consumer deposits and, on the wholesale side, on operational deposits. We have been reducing CDs and higher-cost institutional deposits and other less valuable, one-off types.
deposit_costs.speaker: John Stern, CFO

capital_return.summary: CET1 was 10.8% (9.3% incl. AOCI); buybacks were doubled from $100M to $200M this quarter with further glide-up anticipated, and management reaffirmed a 70%-75% payout target while citing expected RWA relief from Basel III proposals as supportive of returning to historical deployment.
capital_return.quote: The dividend is extremely important, and buybacks — we went from $100 million to $200 million this quarter. I would anticipate we will continue to glide up.
capital_return.speaker: John Stern, CFO

loan_growth.summary: Average loans grew 3.8% YoY (5.3% ex-2025 loan sales) with broad-based organic strength in commercial, credit card, and CRE; management raised its full-year outlook to mid-single digits, plus an incremental ~$1.6B from the Amazon partnership in Q3.
loan_growth.quote: We have talked about loan growth being in that 3% to 4% range, but I certainly think it is going to be higher than that — probably more in the mid-single-digit range — for the full year.
loan_growth.speaker: John Stern, CFO

macro_outlook.summary: Management views the macro backdrop as constructive despite some softening sentiment, citing stable consumer spend, core loan demand, and delinquency trends, with sentiment shifting toward healthy core middle-market demand.
macro_outlook.quote: As we look ahead, the macroeconomic backdrop remains constructive, despite some softening of sentiment recently. Consumer spend, core loan demand, and credit delinquency trends all indicate relative stability.
macro_outlook.speaker: Gunjan Kedia, President and CEO

notable_qa.summary: Gerard Cassidy (RBC) and others pressed management on the NDFI / business credit intermediary portfolio and what scenario would trigger losses; management defended the structures as having very low loss likelihood while acknowledging they 'never say never.'
notable_qa.quote: As bankers, we never say never — that is why we have limits, underwriting practices, and risk management. It is hard to envision the trigger, but rigor and limits are there for that reason.
notable_qa.speaker: John Stern, CFO
